reasoning: The Lean statement `IsOpen U ↔ ∀ x ∈ U, ¬ ClusterPt x (𝓟 Uᶜ)` faithfully expresses "U is open iff none of its points are limits of its complement." In Mathlib, `ClusterPt x (𝓟 Uᶜ)` unfolds (via `clusterPt_principal_iff`) to "every neighborhood of x meets Uᶜ," i.e., x is an adherent/limit point of Uᶜ — the standard topological reading of "p is a limit of the set Uᶜ." The iff direction, the universal quantifier "∀ x ∈ U", and the negation "¬ ClusterPt" correctly encode "none of its points are limits of its complement." The generalization from the metric-space setting to `TopologicalSpace M` is the natural abstract setting for this characterization (U open ⇔ Uᶜ closed ⇔ Uᶜ = closure Uᶜ ⇔ no point of U lies in closure Uᶜ), and it captures exactly the same mathematical content.
